reflection: Session = hold day. Portfolio is at the 5-position concurrent max, regime is strong, and every position is green with no thesis breaks. The correct action is to do nothing — resisting the urge to churn or swap positions is itself a discipline. Evidence-backed rules don't apply today since I'm not entering. Key watch items going forward: (1) LITE is 11.78% off its 52w high — if it continues weakening while the others advance, it may be the swap candidate when a fresh Super Stock setup appears. (2) TER is a Super Stock at new highs — resist the urge to trim a winner. (3) VIX ticked up; if it punches above 22 with SPY breaking MA50, shift defensive. No new rule warranted — 'don't trade when at max positions' is already encoded in the baseline rulebook and I followed it correctly today.
